Caption: Schematic illustration of the reaction mechanism and application of Cu/Fe3O4.

(a) Schematic illustration of the synthesis process of Cu/Fe3O4. (b) SEM image of Cu2O/Fe(OH)3. (c) SEM image of Cu/Fe3O4. (d) XRD patterns of Cu(OH)2, Cu2O, and Cu/Fe3O4. (e) TEM image of Cu/Fe3O4. (f) HRTEM image of Cu/Fe3O4. (g) TEM elemental mapping of Cu/Fe3O4. (h) Fe 2p and (i) Cu 2p XPS spectra of Cu/Fe3O4 (Insert: Cu LMM Auger spectrum). (j) Magnetic hysteresis loop of Cu/Fe3O4.

(a) Comparison of adsorption energy of Li on Cu and Cu/Fe3O4 surfaces. (b,c) The adsorption energy and the optimized adsorption structures with one Li atom on (b) Cu/Fe3O4 and (c) Cu. (d,e) The differential charge density for a single Li atom adsorption on (d) Cu/Fe3O4 and (e) Cu. (f,g) The work function of (f) Cu and (g) Fe3O4. (h) Schematic diagram of the built-in electric field between Fe3O4 and Cu. (i) Differential charge density of Fe3O4 and Cu.

(a) CE of Li||Cu/Fe3O4 cell. (Insert is the CE of randomly selected cycles). (b) Voltage profiles of Li||Cu/Fe3O4 cell at different cycles. (The inserted figure is its enlarged view). (c) Nucleation overpotentials of Li on Cu/Fe3O4 and bare Cu. (d) Tafel curves of Li-Cu/Fe3O4, Li-Cu and bare Li. (e) Nyquist plots of Cu/Fe3O4 asymmetric battery under different cycle numbers. (The inserted figure is its enlarged view). (f) The comparison of the electrochemical impedance of Cu/Fe3O4 and bare Cu electrodes at different cycle numbers. (g,i) Voltage hysteresis of Li plating/stripping in the Li-Cu and Li-Cu/Fe3O4 symmetric cell at a current density of (g) 2 mA cm−2 for 1 mAh cm−2 and (i) 1 mA cm−2 for 1 mAh cm−2. (h) Rate performance of symmetric cells based on the Li/Cu and Li-Cu/Fe3O4 electrodes at different current densities with a capacity limited to 1 mAh cm−2.
